name: NMEA 0183
description: Serial navigation protocol for marine electronics integration, transmitting GPS, compass, depth, and speed data at 4800-38400 bps.

name: NMEA 2000
description: CAN-based marine networking standard enabling real-time data exchange between navigational, engine, and safety systems at up to 250 kbps.

name: AIS (Automatic Identification System)
description: VHF-based automatic position and course reporting system for maritime collision avoidance and vessel tracking, operating on 161.975/162.025 MHz.

name: GMDSS (Global Maritime Distress)
description: International maritime safety system for distress alerts via satellite and terrestrial networks on multiple frequencies (156-174 MHz, 1606-1644 kHz).

name: Inmarsat Maritime
description: Geostationary satellite communication system for global maritime voice, data, and messaging via L-band (1525-1559 MHz and 1626.5-1660.5 MHz).

name: VSAT Maritime
description: Very Small Aperture Terminal satellite system for broadband internet and data on vessels using Ku/Ka-band (11-14 GHz uplink, 10-12 GHz downlink).

name: Fleet Broadband
description: Inmarsat service providing broadband connectivity up to 384 kbps for ships, integrating voice, email, and video over L-band satellite.

name: Iridium Certus Maritime
description: LEO satellite system providing global broadband connectivity up to 1.4 Mbps for remote maritime operations via L-band (1616-1626.5 MHz).

name: VHF Maritime Radio (DSC)
description: VHF maritime voice communication (156-162 MHz) with Digital Selective Calling for automated distress, urgency, and safety messaging.

name: MF/HF Maritime Radio
description: Medium and high frequency maritime radio for long-range communication (500 kHz-28 MHz), enabling ship-to-shore and ship-to-ship contact beyond VHF range.

name: Acoustic Modem (Underwater)
description: Acoustic modem for subsea data transmission at 5-40 kHz, enabling submarine, ROV, and diver communication with ranges up to 10km.

name: Acoustic Positioning (USBL/LBL/SBL)
description: Ultra-Short Baseline, Long Baseline, and Short Baseline acoustic positioning systems for precise underwater vehicle and object location at 100-500 kHz.

name: Underwater Acoustic Communication
description: High-frequency acoustic communication network for scientific research, telemetry, and command between subsea instruments and platforms.

name: Subsea Ethernet (Wet-Mate)
description: Wet-mateable subsea Ethernet connector system providing 100/1000 Mbps connectivity for ROVs, AUVs, and offshore infrastructure at high pressure.

name: Subsea Fiber Optic
description: High-capacity submarine fiber optic cables for inter-continental telecommunications, offering 10-400 Tbps with minimal latency at global scale.

name: ROV Tether Communication
description: Electro-optic tether providing power and high-speed bidirectional communication to remotely operated vehicles at depths up to 6000m.

name: AUV Communication Link
description: Wireless acoustic and optical link for autonomous underwater vehicles enabling telemetry, navigation commands, and sensor data recovery.

name: Sonar Data Link
description: Integrated sonar system communication for transmitting real-time acoustic imaging and target data between sensors and processing stations.

name: Submarine ELF Communication
description: Extremely low frequency submarine communication at 76 Hz and 82 Hz, enabling strategic command messaging to deeply submerged vessels.

name: Submarine VLF Communication
description: Very low frequency transmission at 15-35 kHz for submarine command, control, and navigation signals with global propagation range.

name: Ship Area Network (ShipLAN)
description: Integrated ship network combining navigation, propulsion, cargo, and safety systems over Ethernet with NMEA 2000 backbone.

name: Maritime Mesh Network
description: Self-healing mesh network for ship-to-ship and ship-to-shore communication using VHF/UHF, enabling data sharing and relay without central infrastructure.

name: Maritime 4G/LTE
description: LTE cellular network for vessels within coastal and maritime zones, providing broadband connectivity with fallback to 3G/satellite.

name: Offshore Platform Network
description: Integrated wireless mesh network for oil/gas platforms combining process control, safety, and communications over multiple frequency bands.

name: VDES (VHF Data Exchange System)
description: Next-generation maritime data system extending AIS capacity with high-bandwidth messaging at 156-162 MHz, supporting 10 times more data traffic.

name: LRIT (Long Range Identification Tracking)
description: International vessel tracking system reporting position via satellite every 6-24 hours, enabling real-time maritime domain awareness.

name: Navtex
description: Automated maritime safety information broadcast at 518 kHz and 4209.5 kHz for weather, navigation warnings, and distress alerts.

name: Digital Selective Calling (DSC)
description: Automated distress calling system for maritime vessels using FSK modulation on HF, MF, and VHF bands with selective addressing.

name: Electronic Chart Display (ECDIS Link)
description: Integrated electronic chart system linking navigation sensors, autopilot, and route planning over Ethernet with S-57 chart data.

name: Vessel Traffic Service (VTS)
description: Coastal authority system for vessel surveillance combining radar, AIS, VHF communications, and electronic chart display for port safety management.

name: Port Management Network
description: Integrated port operations network for cargo handling, vessel scheduling, customs, and safety coordinating multiple systems and stakeholders.

name: Underwater Optical Communication
description: High-speed laser communication for underwater applications using blue-green light at 470 nm, achieving Mbps rates over 100m distances.

name: Diver Communication System
description: Helmet-mounted full-duplex underwater communication providing voice and telemetry between diver and surface support via 10-100 kHz ultrasonic link.

name: IEC 61850 (Substation Automation)
description: International standard for substation communication providing real-time data exchange between protective relays, circuit breakers, and control systems.

name: GOOSE (Generic Object Oriented Substation Event)
description: IEC 61850 real-time protocol for rapid event notification between IEDs with <4ms latency, critical for protective relay coordination.

name: Sampled Values (IEC 61850-9-2)
description: Real-time digitized waveform transmission for power quality monitoring and protection at 4-16 kHz sampling rates with <1ms latency.

name: DNP3 (Distributed Network Protocol)
description: Legacy SCADA protocol for utility automation supporting analog/digital values, events, and file transfer with CRC error checking.

name: IEC 60870-5-101 (Serial Telecontrol)
description: Serial-based SCADA protocol for energy system monitoring and control with master-slave architecture and balanced transmission.

name: IEC 60870-5-104 (IP Telecontrol)
description: TCP/IP-based SCADA protocol extending IEC 60870-5-101 for modern utility networks with client-server architecture and multiple simultaneous connections.

name: IEC 62351 (Security for Power Systems)
description: Cybersecurity standard for power systems defining authentication, encryption, and secure key management for IEC 60870 and IEC 61850.

name: OpenADR (Automated Demand Response)
description: Open standard for real-time energy consumption reduction signals, enabling automated load shedding and price-responsive control.

name: IEEE 2030.5 (Smart Energy Profile)
description: Standard for device communication in smart grid, supporting demand response, pricing, and distributed energy resources management.

name: SEP 2.0 (Smart Energy Profile)
description: ZigBee-based smart grid protocol for meter data collection, pricing signals, and demand response enabling interoperable DER integration.

name: DLMS/COSEM (Metering)
description: Device language message specification for secure meter data exchange supporting various physical layer transports and meter manufacturing flexibility.

name: Modbus for Energy
description: Simple protocol for energy device communication including inverters, meters, and controllers over serial, TCP, and wireless transports.

name: C12.18/C12.19 (ANSI Metering)
description: American standard for meter data exchange with optical port communication, enabling meter reading and programming in North American utilities.

name: OSGP (Open Smart Grid Protocol)
description: Open protocol for meter communication and command delivery supporting multiple transport layers and international utility deployments.

name: IEEE 1901 (Power Line Communication)
description: Broadband over power line standard enabling 200+ Mbps data transmission through electric distribution networks with OFDM modulation.

name: G3-PLC (Smart Grid PLC)
description: IPv6-native narrowband power line communication for advanced metering at 10-100 kbps, optimized for low-power utility devices.

name: PRIME (Powerline Intelligent Metering)
description: European narrowband powerline communication protocol for smart metering with mesh routing, operating at 95-125 kbps.

name: RF Mesh AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure)
description: Wireless mesh network for meter data collection using 900 MHz or licensed spectrum with self-healing topology and multi-hop routing.

name: Wi-SUN (Wireless Smart Utility Network)
description: Sub-GHz wireless mesh for utilities supporting IPv6 and interoperable device communication across frequencies 400-930 MHz.

name: LoRaWAN for Utilities
description: Low-power wide-area network for utility applications at 868/915 MHz, enabling meter data collection and remote disconnect over long distances.

name: NB-IoT for Smart Grid
description: Cellular IoT technology for utility devices providing 250 kbps throughput with extended coverage and reduced device power consumption.

name: Synchrophasor Network (IEEE C37.118)
description: Real-time phasor measurement network for grid monitoring and control, collecting synchronized voltage/current/frequency data at 30-60 Hz.

name: ICCP/TASE.2 (Inter-Control Center Communication)
description: Protocol for secure data exchange between control centers and utilities, enabling coordinated operation and emergency response.

name: CIM (Common Information Model) Network
description: Standardized data model for power system representation enabling interoperable information exchange across utilities and applications.

name: EMS/SCADA Network
description: Energy Management System integrating SCADA for real-time monitoring and control of generation, transmission, and distribution systems.

name: DMS (Distribution Management System) Network
description: Distribution network management platform for voltage optimization, load balancing, and fault detection in medium-voltage networks.

name: OMS (Outage Management System) Network
description: System for tracking and managing power outages, coordinating field crews, and restoring service through automated switching.

name: Microgrid Controller Network
description: Control network for islanding-capable microgrids coordinating DERs, storage, and loads with seamless transition between grid-connected and island modes.

name: EV Charging Network (OCPP)
description: Open Charge Point Protocol for communication between EV chargers and management systems, enabling smart charging and load coordination.

name: Solar Inverter Communication (SunSpec)
description: Standardized Modbus-based protocol for solar PV inverter monitoring and control, enabling grid services and curtailment.

name: Wind Turbine SCADA (IEC 61400-25)
description: Wind energy industry standard for turbine monitoring, diagnostics, and control enabling integration with grid management systems.

name: Battery Management System Network
description: Networked battery storage system for frequency regulation and peak shaving, coordinating charging/discharging with grid signals.

name: Virtual Power Plant Network
description: Aggregation platform coordinating distributed generation, storage, and demand for ancillary services and market participation.

name: CCSDS Proximity-1
description: Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems protocol for low-power spacecraft-to-spacecraft and spacecraft-to-lander communication.

name: CCSDS AOS (Advanced Orbiting Systems)
description: Space data link protocol for satellite missions providing framing, synchronization, and error correction for telemetry and command.

name: CCSDS TM (Telemetry)
description: Standard telemetry protocol for spacecraft data downlink with flexible framing and error correction through convolutional coding.

name: CCSDS TC (Telecommand)
description: Standardized command uplink protocol for spacecraft with ARQ error handling and secure command verification.

name: DTN (Delay-Tolerant Networking)
description: Bundle protocol suite for space networks with extreme latency and intermittent connectivity, enabling store-and-forward messaging.

name: Bundle Protocol (BP)
description: DTN transport protocol for disruption-tolerant networks, bundling data for reliable delivery across time-varying paths.

name: LTP (Licklider Transmission Protocol)
description: Reliable transmission protocol for deep space links with session layer for long-delay networks and sender-directed retransmission.

name: SpaceWire
description: High-speed serial network for spacecraft onboard data handling, providing 2-200 Mbps with packet switching and fault tolerance.

name: SpaceWire-Light
description: Reduced-complexity SpaceWire variant for CubeSats and small satellites, supporting 2-10 Mbps with minimal FPGA resources.

name: SpaceFibre
description: Next-generation spacecraft network supporting 10-20 Gbps optical and electrical links with full-duplex packet switching.

name: TT&C (Telemetry, Tracking, Command)
description: Integrated spacecraft communication system combining telemetry downlink, ranging/tracking, and command uplink with antenna switching.

name: S-Band Communication
description: Microwave band at 2-4 GHz for spacecraft communication, used for proximity operations and lunar missions with 50 dBW transmission.

name: X-Band Communication
description: Microwave band at 8-12 GHz for high-rate interplanetary data transmission, supporting 100+ Mbps for deep space probes.

name: Ka-Band Communication
description: Millimeter-wave band at 32-34 GHz for maximum data rate, enabling gigabit transmission for Mars rovers and outer planet probes.

name: Ku-Band Communication
description: Microwave band at 12-18 GHz for satellite communication, used for Earth orbit spacecraft with 10-100 Mbps capacity.

name: V-Band (60 GHz) Space
description: Millimeter-wave band at 59-64 GHz for high-bandwidth near-Earth satellite links, supporting multi-gigabit rates.

name: Laser Communication (Optical Inter-Satellite Link)
description: Optical inter-satellite link using laser frequencies, enabling 10+ Gbps transmission between spacecraft with minimal power consumption.

name: NASA LCRD (Laser Comm Relay Demo)
description: Demonstration of laser relay network for Earth orbit, enabling multi-gigabit crosslinks and ground station communication.

name: ESA EDRS (European Data Relay)
description: European optical relay satellite system enabling low-Earth orbit spacecraft to downlink via geostationary laser link.

name: Deep Space Network (DSN) Link
description: NASA global ground station network at X/S/Ka-band providing telemetry, tracking, and command for interplanetary spacecraft.

name: Lunar Communication Relay
description: Communication network for lunar surface assets via relay satellites, enabling direct relay from surface to Earth.

name: Mars Communication Relay
description: Relay satellite network for Mars surface rovers enabling direct communication via orbital relays with variable 3-22 minute latency.

name: Inter-Satellite Link (ISL)
description: Radio or optical link between satellites enabling packet switching, load balancing, and resilience without ground station dependence.

name: Starlink Inter-Satellite Laser
description: Optical inter-satellite link for Starlink constellation enabling multi-terabit backhaul without ground gateway dependency.

name: CubeSat Communication (UHF/VHF)
description: Low-power UHF/VHF transceiver for CubeSat swarms enabling local coordination and data sharing at 400-470 MHz.

name: SmallSat S-Band
description: S-band transceiver for small satellites enabling command/control and telemetry at 2-4 GHz with 100 kbps-1 Mbps.

name: GPS L-Band Data Link
description: Dual-use navigation and communication link at L1/L5 frequencies for spacecraft receiving GPS-like signals for navigation and data.

name: Galileo Data Link
description: European satellite navigation system signal at 1.2 GHz enabling dual-use positioning and data reception for autonomous spacecraft.

name: TDRS (Tracking & Data Relay Satellite)
description: NASA geostationary relay satellite network providing continuous communication for low-Earth orbit spacecraft at S/Ku-band.

name: Artemis Lunar Gateway Network
description: Integrated communication network for lunar orbit gateway station coordinating lunar surface, LEO relay, and Earth communications.
